title: US CENTCOM Reports 49th Vessel Redirected Under Iran Port Blockade Enforcement
summary: US Central Command announced the 49th commercial vessel redirection under its counter-blockade of Iranian ports, reaffirming 'total enforcement' commitment as Strait of Hormuz navigation reportedly halted.
contents:
## US Naval Blockade Enforcement Update

### CENTCOM Announcement
US Central Command (CENTCOM) declared on May 3, 2026 that 49 commercial vessels have now undergone redirection to comply with the American counter-blockade of Iranian ports. "US forces remain fully committed to total enforcement," CENTCOM stated via social media.

### Strait of Hormuz Navigation Halt
According to Bloomberg data cited by TASS, navigation through the Strait of Hormuz nearly ceased on May 3 following Trump's comments about potential conflict resumption. Only two Iranian commercial vessels transited the strait on Sunday, compared to nine vessels the previous day.

### Blockade Context
The US imposed the counter-blockade on Iranian ports on April 13, shortly after a fragile ceasefire took effect. Iran has maintained effective control over the strait since the war commenced on February 28, demanding tolls from non-US, non-Israeli vessels seeking passage.

### Iranian Position
Iran's Deputy Parliament Speaker Ali Nikzad declared from Larak Island that Iran "will not back down from our position on the Strait of Hormuz, and it will not return to its prewar conditions." He characterized Trump's blockade plan as "certain to fail" and reiterated that non-US, non-Israeli ships may pass after paying a toll.

### Impact on Global Trade
The US has warned shipping companies they face sanctions for paying Iran in any form, including digital assets. An Iranian supertanker carrying 1.9 million barrels of crude oil (valued at $220 million) reportedly evaded the blockade and reached the Asia Pacific.
